contents.label: Thirty-ninth sermon: We ought to build a home free of rot by taking up moral virtues
Fortieth sermon: We ought not to extend our battles beyond our power for the sake of God
Forty-second sermon: On concord and love
Forty-third sermon: We ought to spend our days of holy fasting in rest and ease
Forty-fourth sermon: On sudden departure from this worldly life
Forty-fifth sermon: We ought not to surrender ourselves to adversity
Forty-sixth sermon: We ought to imitate the suffering of the Lord
Forty-seventh sermon: Report on the Christians killed in Bolghan for their refusal to eat meat during Lent
Forty-eighth sermon: On the dominion of our Lord Jesus Christ and his incarnation
Forty-ninth sermon: On the Annunciation to the Virgin Mary
Fiftieth sermon: The Passover prefigures the future, eternal Passover in patience and courage
Fifty-first sermon: On always celebrating and keeping the Passover for the sake of God
Fifty-second sermon: We ought to be renewed after having received news of our coming trials, both perceptible and imperceptible
Fifty-third sermon: We ought to endure any punishment in imitation of Christ and his holiness
Fifty-fourth sermon: We ought to guard ourselves from the deception of Satan
Fifty-fifth sermon: On righteous suffering and the lessons of humility and patience
Fifty-sixth sermon: On the suffering of our Lord and Master Jesus Christ
Fifty-seventh sermon: On the rebuke of brothers who have transgressed and a lesson and sermon on discipline
